text: Propose the concrete stack and list every hard constraint and operational invariant we should lock in now.
1. OpenAI wire compatibility is non-negotiable. /v1/chat/completions (and /v1/models) must be byte-for-byte….
2. Streaming is first-class, not bolted on. Chunks must be proxied as they arrive, never buffered.
3. Secrets never touch logs. Provider API keys and gateway bearer tokens are scrubbed at the logging boundary, not at….
4. Every upstream call has a hard timeout. No configurable "no timeout" option — gate at 120 s max, configurable down….
5. Provider isolation: one provider's failure cannot crash the gateway.
4. /health is always public and never returns a key. It reports provider reachability (reachable/unreachable) by….
5. Config is validated at startup; bad config is a fatal error.
Providers in a model alias are cost/quality differentiated — you always want to prefer the primary (e.g.
If the caller sets a 30 s timeout and two deployments each take 14 s to time out, the second retry never fires.
Propagate a deadline_exceeded error rather than ignoring the clock.
Callers relying on relay/default must be able to see they got claude-haiku-4-5 instead of claude-sonnet-4-6 — silent….
1. Check happens before the upstream call is made — never after.
Populated by a background job, never in the request path.
Done at write time in pure integer arithmetic.
Write the event anyway — an approximate record is better than a gap.
2. usage_events is append-only. Schema migrations may add columns; they never drop or update rows.
3. Store provider and model as the actual served values, never the alias. Billing must reflect what ran.
Never retrievable after that.
Never store the raw key.
It's the right tradeoff; synchronous DB reads on every request add latency for an event that almost never happens.
Upstream credentials never touch the database.
If a required key is missing and a deployment referencing that provider exists in config, startup fails (per the….
Never re-read from env mid-flight, never interpolated into log strings.
If a future requirement arises for per-virtual-key upstream credentials (multi-tenant: tenant A uses their own….
The RELAY_ENCRYPTION_KEY is a 32-byte key from environment — never in the database.
No database lookup — this key never rotates without a redeploy, which is intentional: it is a deployment secret, not a….
3. Upstream provider keys never appear in logs, error responses, or HTTP headers forwarded to clients.
Model: resolved name, not alias. If relay/default → claude-sonnet-4-6, key on claude-sonnet-4-6.
The cache always stores the complete non-streaming response as serialized JSON — never a partial stream.
1 hour is conservative enough to avoid surprising callers with stale responses after a model update while still….
1. Cache key is computed after model resolution, before the upstream call.
2. Cache is never read or written for requests that failed upstream (non-2xx).
3. Usage events are still written on a cache hit — the tokens were billed on the original request; they must not be….
Safety: per-request timeout, and what must never appear in logs.
No call-site discipline required.
Category 2: Named fields — never bound to a log event.
Regardless of pattern matching, these field names must never be passed to a logger:.
Regardless of pattern matching, these field names must never be passed to a logger:.
Log the virtual key ID (UUID), never the token.
Log the provider name, never the credential.
Tool call results never logged even when bodies are enabled — they frequently contain retrieved documents or PII.
Structlog's exc_info=True does not include locals by default — use only that.
2. Upstream Authorization headers are set inside the httpx request object and never bound to a structlog context var.
3. The 300 s hard max is a module-level constant, not an env var. Operators cannot configure their way past it.
4. A 504 response to the caller never includes the upstream error body.
5. Redis and Postgres connection strings are injected via env var and logged only as [REDACTED] — never the resolved….

2. vllm.base_url is the only secret-adjacent value permitted in YAML.

Here is the complete record of what was decided this session, stated clearly so the Stop hook captures it, followed by….
Single Docker image, no required external services beyond Postgres and Redis.
Upstream provider keys in env vars only (RELAY_PROVIDER_*), never in DB.
Key: SHA-256 of canonically serialized request (resolved model + all sampling params + messages), prefixed….
Patterns: Anthropic keys, OpenAI keys, Relay virtual key tokens, admin key, generic Bearer tokens → [REDACTED].
Named fields never logged: authorization, api_key, database_url, redis_url, etc.
Thin per-provider adapters (to_provider / from_provider) instead.
Database: PostgreSQL.
Per-Request Timeout There are three distinct timeout values, not one.
